1.1: How deserted she sits, the city once full of people! She has become like a widow. Once great among the nations, once a princess among the provinces, she is now forced into servitude.
1.2: She weeps bitterly in the night, her tears on her cheeks. Among all who loved her there is none to comfort her; all her friends have betrayed her and become her enemies.
1.3: Judah has gone into exile because of affliction and hard servitude. She lives among the nations but finds no resting place. All her pursuers have overtaken her in the midst of her straits.
1.4: The roads to Zion mourn because no one comes to her appointed feasts. All her gates are desolate; her priests groan; her young women are sorrowful; she herself is in bitter distress.
1.5: Her adversaries have become her masters; her enemies prosper, because the LORD afflicted her for the multitude of her transgressions. Her children have been led away captive before the enemy.
1.6: And from the daughter of Zion all her majesty has departed. Her princes have become like deer that find no pasture; they fled exhausted before the pursuer.
1.7: Jerusalem recalled in the days of her affliction and exile all the precious things she once possessed in former days. When her people fell into the hand of the foe with no one to help, the adversaries looked on and laughed at her destruction.
1.8: Jerusalem sinned gravely; therefore she has become unclean. All who once honored her now despise her, for they have seen her shame. She herself groans and turns away.
1.9: Her uncleanness clung to her skirts; she did not consider her future. Her fall was appalling, with no one to comfort her. 'See, O LORD, my affliction, for the enemy has triumphed!'
1.10: The enemy stretched out his hand over all her treasures. She watched the nations enter her sanctuary — nations that you had commanded should not enter your congregation.
1.11: All her people groan as they search for bread. They traded their treasures for food to keep themselves alive. 'Look, O LORD, and consider — how contemptible I have become!'
1.12: Is it nothing to you, all who pass by? Look and see if there is any sorrow like my sorrow, the sorrow brought upon me, with which the LORD afflicted me on the day of his burning anger.
1.13: From on high he sent fire into my bones. He spread a net for my feet and turned me back. He has left me desolate and faint all day long.
1.14: The yoke of my transgressions was tightly bound; braided together by his hand and placed on my neck, it sapped my strength. The Lord has handed me over to those I cannot resist.
1.15: The Lord dismissed all my warriors from my midst. He called up a host against me to crush my young men. The Lord has trodden the virgin daughter of Judah as in a winepress.
1.16: For these things I weep; my eyes stream with tears because no comforter is near who could revive my spirit. My children are desolate, for the enemy has triumphed.
1.17: Zion stretches out her hands, but there is no one to comfort her. The LORD has commanded that Jacob's adversaries encircle him. Jerusalem has become a thing of impurity in their midst.
1.18: The LORD is in the right, for I rebelled against his command. Hear, all peoples, and look at my anguish. My young women and young men have gone into captivity.
1.19: I called to my allies, but they deceived me. My priests and my elders perished in the city as they searched for food to keep themselves alive.
1.20: See, O LORD, how distressed I am! My insides churn; my heart writhes within me, for I have sinned deeply. In the streets the sword kills; inside it is scarcely better — death either way.
1.21: They heard my sighing; there is no one to comfort me. All my enemies heard of my misery and are glad that you brought this about. Bring on the day you announced; let them become as I am.
1.22: Let all their wickedness come before you, and deal with them as you have dealt with me for all my transgressions. For my sighs are many and my heart is faint.

2.1: How the Lord shrouded the daughter of Zion in his anger! He hurled the splendor of Israel down from heaven to earth. He did not remember his footstool on the day of his burning anger.
2.2: The Lord swallowed up all Jacob's dwellings without pity. In his wrath he broke down the strongholds of Judah's daughter; he brought the kingdom and its princes down to the ground in disgrace.
2.3: In fierce anger he cut off every source of Israel's strength. He withdrew his right hand in the face of the enemy. He blazed through Jacob like a fire consuming everything around it.
2.4: He bent his bow like an enemy; he stood firm as an adversary and cut down all who were pleasing to the eye. He poured out his wrath like fire into the tent of Zion's daughter.
2.5: The Lord has acted like an enemy. He has swallowed up Israel, swallowed all her palaces. He demolished his own strongholds and multiplied mourning and lamentation in Judah's daughter.
2.6: He broke down his tabernacle like a garden shelter and ruined his assembly place. The LORD made the appointed feasts and sabbaths in Zion forgotten; in his fierce anger he rejected both king and priest.
2.7: The Lord rejected his altar and disowned his sanctuary. He handed over the walls of her palaces to the enemy. They raised a shout in the house of the LORD as on a feast day.
2.8: The LORD planned the destruction of Zion's wall. He stretched out the measuring line and would not pull his hand back from demolishing. He caused rampart and wall to mourn; they wasted away together.
2.9: Her gates have sunk into the ground; he shattered and broke their bars. Her king and princes are among the nations; the law is gone, and her prophets receive no vision from the LORD.
2.10: The elders of Zion's daughter sit on the ground in silence. They have thrown dust on their heads and put on sackcloth. Jerusalem's young women bow their heads to the ground.
2.11: My eyes are spent with weeping; my insides churn; my bile spills on the ground over the destruction of my people, because infants and nursing babies collapse in the city streets.
2.12: They cry to their mothers, 'Where is grain and wine?' as they collapse like the mortally wounded in the city streets, their breath ebbing away on their mothers' laps.
2.13: What can I say to you? To what can I compare you, daughter of Jerusalem? To what shall I liken you to offer comfort, virgin daughter of Zion? Your wound is as vast as the sea. Who can heal you?
2.14: Your prophets gave you empty and deceptive visions. They did not expose your sin to restore you. The oracles they proclaimed were false and misleading.
2.15: All who pass by clap their hands at you; they hiss and wag their heads at Jerusalem's daughter: 'Is this the city that was called the perfection of beauty, the joy of all the earth?'
2.16: All your enemies open wide their mouths against you; they hiss and gnash their teeth. They say, 'We have devoured her! This is the day we waited for! We have seen it — it has come!'
2.17: The LORD has done what he planned; he fulfilled the word he decreed in days of old. He overthrew without pity and let your enemy rejoice over you; he raised up the strength of your adversaries.
2.18: Their heart cried out to the Lord. O wall of Zion's daughter, let tears flow like a river day and night! Give yourself no rest; let your eyes run without ceasing!
2.19: Arise, cry out in the night! At the start of the night watches pour out your heart like water before the Lord. Lift your hands to him for the lives of your children, who are fainting from hunger at the head of every street.
2.20: Look, O LORD, and take heed! To whom have you done this? Should women eat the fruit of their own wombs, their newborn children? Should priest and prophet be killed in the LORD's sanctuary?
2.21: Young and old lie on the ground in the streets. My young women and young men have fallen by the sword. You killed them in the day of your anger; you showed no mercy.
2.22: You called my terrors from every side as though summoning people to a feast day. On the day of the LORD's anger, none escaped or survived. Those I had held and raised, my enemy destroyed.

3.1: I am the man who has seen affliction under the rod of God's wrath.
3.2: He has driven me and brought me into darkness, not light.
3.3: He has turned his hand against me time after time, all day long.
3.4: He has worn away my flesh and skin; he has broken my bones.
3.5: He has besieged and encircled me with gall and tribulation.
3.6: He has made me dwell in darkness like those long dead.
3.7: He has walled me in so I cannot escape; he has made my chains heavy.
3.8: Even when I cry out and shout, he shuts out my prayer.
3.9: He has blocked my paths with cut stone; he has made my ways crooked.
3.10: He was like a bear lying in ambush, like a lion in hiding.
3.11: He turned me off my path and tore me apart; he has left me desolate.
3.12: He bent his bow and set me as a target for his arrows.
3.13: He shot the arrows of his quiver into my inmost being.
3.14: I have become a laughingstock to all my people, the object of their mockery all day long.
3.15: He has filled me with bitterness; he has satiated me with wormwood.
3.16: He ground my teeth with gravel and made me cower in ashes.
3.17: My soul has been stripped of peace; I have forgotten what happiness is.
3.18: And I said, 'My endurance has perished, and my hope from the LORD.'
3.19: Remembering my affliction and my wandering, the wormwood and the gall.
3.20: My soul continually remembers it and sinks down within me.
3.21: Yet this I call to mind, and therefore I have hope:
3.22: It is because of the LORD's steadfast love that we are not consumed, for his mercies never fail.
3.23: They are new every morning. Great is your faithfulness!
3.24: The LORD is my portion, says my soul; therefore I will hope in him.
3.25: The LORD is good to those who wait for him, to the soul that seeks him.
3.26: It is good for a person to wait quietly for the salvation of the LORD.
3.27: It is good for a man to bear the yoke in his youth.
3.28: Let him sit alone and in silence when God has placed it on him.
3.29: Let him put his mouth in the dust — there may still be hope.
3.30: Let him offer his cheek to the one who strikes him and be filled with insults.
3.31: For the Lord will not reject forever.
3.32: Though he causes grief, he will have compassion in accordance with the abundance of his steadfast love.
3.33: For he does not willingly afflict or grieve the children of men.
3.34: To crush beneath his feet all the prisoners of the earth,
3.35: to deny a man his rights before the Most High —
3.36: to deprive a man of justice in his own case — the Lord does not approve of these things.
3.37: Who has spoken and it came to pass, unless the Lord has commanded it?
3.38: Is it not from the mouth of the Most High that both good and evil come?
3.39: Why should a living man complain — about the punishment of his own sins?
3.40: Let us examine and test our ways and turn back to the LORD.
3.41: Let us lift up our hearts and hands together to God in heaven.
3.42: We have transgressed and rebelled, and you have not forgiven.
3.43: You wrapped yourself in anger and pursued us; you killed without pity.
3.44: You have veiled yourself in a cloud so that no prayer can pass through.
3.45: You have made us refuse and scum among the peoples.
3.46: All our enemies have opened wide their mouths against us.
3.47: Panic and pitfall have come upon us, devastation and destruction.
3.48: My eyes stream with rivers of tears because of the destruction of my people.
3.49: My eyes will keep flowing without ceasing, without rest,
3.50: until the LORD looks down from heaven and sees.
3.51: My eyes bring pain to my heart because of all the daughters of my city.
3.52: Those who are my enemies without cause hunted me like a bird.
3.53: They cut short my life in the pit and threw stones upon me.
3.54: Water closed over my head; I said, 'I am finished.'
3.55: I called on your name, LORD, from the deepest pit.
3.56: You heard my voice. Do not turn away your ear from my cry, from my breathing.
3.57: You drew near when I called on you; you said, 'Fear not.'
3.58: Lord, you have taken up my case; you have redeemed my life.
3.59: LORD, you have seen the wrong done to me. Uphold my cause.
3.60: You have seen all their vengeance, all their plots against me.
3.61: You have heard their taunts, LORD, all their scheming against me,
3.62: the lips of my attackers and their scheming against me all day long.
3.63: Look at them — whether they sit or stand, I am the subject of their mockery.
3.64: You will repay them, LORD, according to what their hands have done.
3.65: Give them anguish of heart and your curse upon them.
3.66: Pursue them in anger and destroy them from under your heavens, LORD.

4.1: How the gold has lost its luster, how the finest gold is tarnished! The sacred stones lie scattered at the head of every street.
4.2: The precious sons of Zion, once worth their weight in gold, are now counted as earthen pottery, the work of a potter's hands.
4.3: Even jackals bare their breasts and nurse their young, but my people's daughter has become cruel, like ostriches in the wilderness.
4.4: The nursing infant's tongue cleaves to the roof of his mouth for thirst. Young children beg for bread, but no one has any to break for them.
4.5: Those who feasted on fine food lie destitute in the streets. Those raised in royal purple now embrace ash heaps.
4.6: The punishment for the iniquity of my people's daughter is greater than the penalty for Sodom's sin, which was overthrown in an instant with no human hands against her.
4.7: Her dedicated ones were purer than snow, whiter than milk; their bodies more ruddy than coral, their bearing like sapphire.
4.8: Now their appearance is blacker than soot; they are not known in the streets. Their skin has shriveled onto their bones, dried hard as wood.
4.9: Better were those killed by the sword than those killed by famine. These waste away, run through with hunger for lack of produce from the fields.
4.10: Compassionate women boiled their own children with their own hands. Their children became food during the destruction of my people's daughter.
4.11: The LORD fulfilled his wrath; he poured out his fierce anger. He kindled a fire in Zion that consumed her foundations.
4.12: The kings of the earth and all the world's inhabitants would not have believed that foe or enemy could enter the gates of Jerusalem.
4.13: This came because of the sins of her prophets and the iniquities of her priests, who shed the blood of the righteous in her midst.
4.14: They wandered through the streets like blind men, so defiled with blood that no one was able to touch their garments.
4.15: 'Away! Unclean!' people shouted at them. 'Away! Away! Do not touch us!' When they fled and wandered, the nations declared, 'They will not dwell among us anymore.'
4.16: The LORD's presence scattered them; he will look on them no longer. No honor was shown to the priests, no favor to the elders.
4.17: Our eyes strained in vain for help. We kept watch for a nation that could not save us.
4.18: They hunted our steps so that we could not walk in our streets. Our end drew near, our days were fulfilled; our end had arrived.
4.19: Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles of the sky. They pursued us through the mountains; they lay in wait for us in the wilderness.
4.20: The LORD's anointed, the very breath of our nostrils, was caught in their pits — the one of whom we said, 'Under his shadow we shall live among the nations.'
4.21: Rejoice and celebrate, daughter of Edom, you who live in the land of Uz! But the cup will come to you as well; you will drink it and expose yourself in your shame.
4.22: Your punishment is complete, daughter of Zion; he will not send you into exile again. But your iniquity will be punished, daughter of Edom; he will expose your sins.

5.1: Remember, O LORD, what has happened to us. Look and see our disgrace.
5.2: Our inheritance has been given over to strangers, our houses to foreigners.
5.3: We have become fatherless orphans; our mothers are as widows.
5.4: We pay money for the water we drink; our wood must be bought at cost.
5.5: Our pursuers are at our necks; we are driven hard with no rest.
5.6: We gave our hand to Egypt and to Assyria in order to get enough bread.
5.7: Our fathers sinned and are gone; we carry the weight of their iniquities.
5.8: Slaves rule over us; there is no one to rescue us from their power.
5.9: We risk our lives to get our bread, because of the sword that threatens in the open country.
5.10: Our skin burns hot as an oven because of the fever brought on by famine.
5.11: Women have been violated in Zion, young women in the towns of Judah.
5.12: Princes have been hanged by their hands; no honor is shown to the faces of elders.
5.13: Young men are set to work at the grinding stone; boys stagger under loads of wood.
5.14: The elders have left the city gate; the young men have abandoned their music.
5.15: The joy of our hearts has ended; our dancing has turned into mourning.
5.16: The crown has fallen from our head. Woe to us — we have sinned!
5.17: Because of this our heart has grown faint; for all these things our eyes have become dim.
5.18: On account of Mount Zion, which lies desolate, jackals prowl across it.
5.19: But you, O LORD, reign forever; your throne endures through all generations.
5.20: Why have you forgotten us forever? Why have you forsaken us for such a long time?
5.21: Restore us to yourself, O LORD, that we may be restored. Renew our days as in former times.
5.22: Or have you utterly rejected us? Are you exceedingly angry with us without end?
